formulas.name: The Rattrap Law - The Power of Human Instinct
formulas.description: Human instincts can be more powerful than reason and intellect.
formulas.notes: This is a key statement highlighting the central idea of the chapter.

formulas.name: The Nature of Human Nature - Dualism
formulas.description: Humans have both good and evil instincts, which often conflict with each other.
formulas.notes: This concept is crucial in understanding the protagonist's actions and the overall theme of the chapter.

formulas.name: The Importance of Instinct in Decision Making
formulas.description: Instinct can play a significant role in guiding human decisions, often overriding rational thought.
formulas.notes: This is a key takeaway from the chapter, emphasizing the significance of instinct in human behavior.

formulas.name: The Duality of Human Existence
formulas.description: Human beings are capable of both good and evil, reflecting the duality of human existence.
formulas.notes: This concept is central to the chapter and highlights the complexity of human nature.

formulas.name: The Limitations of Human Reason
formulas.description: Human reason can be limited in its ability to understand and control human emotions and instincts.
formulas.notes: This is a key statement that underscores the limitations of human reason in the face of powerful emotions and instincts.
